summary: Move uncached structural project-symbol indexing off the renderer thread, expand ordinary-file coverage, preserve bounded exact-source reuse, and replay repeated textual includes without weakening cycle or work limits.
specSections: Section 2.7 test-first evidence and milestone gates

decisions.decision: Run uncached structural project-file indexing in a dedicated Vite Web Worker behind an asynchronous CodeMirror CompletionSource. Keep lazy path-by-path source requests and a bounded normalized-path-plus-exact-source cache; allow 2,100,000 code units per file and 8,000,000 per traversal, with hard file, reference, event, symbol, depth, and 16,000,000-code-unit cache budgets. When Worker is unavailable or fails, use the same structural grammar through a cooperatively yielding fallback, and make abort, worker error, and editor disposal settle safely.
decisions.rationale: The earlier synchronous 50,000-per-file and 100,000-aggregate limits protected the renderer by silently excluding ordinary valid project symbols. Off-renderer parsing preserves responsiveness while materially expanding useful coverage; the cooperative fallback and explicit lifecycle bounds keep unsupported or failed Worker environments safe.
specBasis: Section 2.7 test-first evidence and milestone gates

decisions.decision: Replay cached structural events for every textual include/use occurrence in depth-first source order, using only the active recursion stack for cycle prevention while global source, reference, event, symbol, file, and depth budgets bound repetition and expansion.
decisions.rationale: A global visited set incorrectly made the first occurrence permanent: include A; include B; include A left B authoritative. Replaying already-parsed events restores textual last-occurrence authority without reparsing unchanged source, while stack-local cycle detection and global budgets prevent recursive or repeated graphs from looping or exploding.
specBasis: Section 2.7 test-first evidence and milestone gates

testEvidence.criterion: Previously omitted ordinary files, renderer responsiveness, repeated-include authority, and Worker ownership reproduce before implementation
testEvidence.test: Require A/B/A to restore A, index the final symbol after more than 100,000 aggregate code units, index a two-million-code-unit file after an event-loop heartbeat, and construct/dispose a dedicated project-index Worker.

red.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/ui/editor/openscad-completion.test.ts --reporter=verbose -t "replays cached|former 100,000|two-million|owns and disposes"
red.outcome: ASSERTION-LEVEL RED: Test Files 1 failed; Tests 4 failed and 19 skipped. A/B/A returned B, the sixth ordinary file was absent, the heartbeat had not run, and no Worker was constructed.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/ui/editor/openscad-completion.test.ts --reporter=verbose -t "replays cached|former 100,000|two-million|owns and disposes"
green.outcome: GREEN: Test Files 1 passed; Tests 4 passed and 19 skipped. The two-million-code-unit fallback case completed in 36 ms with the scheduled heartbeat observed.

testEvidence.criterion: Worker result, lazy lookup, error fallback, cancellation, disposal, and aggregate bounds are load-bearing
testEvidence.test: Drive the worker message protocol directly, force a worker error into cooperative structural fallback, abort an unfinished request, and traverse one million-code-unit files until the eight-million aggregate ceiling.

red.outcome: ASSERTION-LEVEL RED: Test Files 1 failed and 1 passed; Tests 3 failed and 1 passed. The injected Worker was bypassed, so result, error-termination, and cancel-message assertions failed while the aggregate ceiling already held.

green.outcome: GREEN: Test Files 2 passed; Tests 4 passed. Worker result/lazy lookup, error fallback/termination, cancellation, disposal, and the eight-file aggregate boundary passed together.

testEvidence.criterion: Rejected sources cannot bypass a hard budget by being referenced twice, and worker-declared errors retire the failed worker
testEvidence.test: Reference the first over-budget file twice after exactly eight million accepted code units, and return the worker's explicit project-index-error protocol response before structural fallback.

red.outcome: ASSERTION-LEVEL RED: Test Files 2 failed; Tests 2 failed and 2 passed. The second reference parsed the previously rejected ninth file, and an explicit worker error fell back without terminating the failed worker.

green.outcome: GREEN: Test Files 2 passed; Tests 4 passed. Rejected paths remain rejected for the request, the exact aggregate ceiling holds under replay, and both worker error channels retire the worker before cooperative fallback.

testEvidence.criterion: Worker posting failures settle all concurrent requests and over-limit files never cross the renderer-to-worker clone boundary
testEvidence.test: Leave one Worker request pending, throw while posting the next request, and separately request a 2,100,001-code-unit source through the Worker protocol.

red.outcome: ASSERTION-LEVEL RED: Test Files 1 failed; Tests 2 failed and 3 passed. The oversized string was posted into the Worker and the earlier pending request remained unsettled after the later post failure.

green.outcome: GREEN: Test Files 1 passed; Tests 5 passed. The client substitutes undefined before cloning an over-limit source and retires the worker into cooperative fallback for every outstanding request after a post failure.

questions: D-0199 supersedes D-0194's implementation limits; D-0194 remains immutable historical evidence of the prior synchronous safety response.
The hard limits affect only supplemental cross-file suggestions; editing, saving, rendering, exports, current-file completion, and project content remain unchanged.
No owner similarity gate, comparison repository, workflow content, or prohibited source was opened or executed.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
